title: The Secret Agent Golden Globe Triumph - American Prospect
text: Brazilian film The Secret Agent achieved major international success at the Golden Globes, winning Best Foreign Film and Best Actor. Director Kleber Mendonça Filho and star Wagner Moura took home the honors for this political thriller about resistance during Brazil's 1970s dictatorship. Moura became the first Brazilian to win the Golden Globe for Best Actor for his portrayal of a fugitive professor. The film has also secured four Oscar nominations, including Best Picture and Best Actor. The movie's success reflects Brazil's government investment strategy under President Lula. His administration has channeled hundreds of millions into cinema subsidies and tax incentives to support anti-fascist storytelling. The film's international tour is sponsored by Petrobras, Brazil's state-owned oil company, amplifying the government's cultural diplomacy efforts.
